briefs.title: Gilmore Diversion Shelter Must Submit Good Neighbour and Operations Plans Before Opening
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has required BC Housing and the shelter operator at 3020 Gilmore to submit a Good Neighbour Plan and an Operational Management Plan before the shelter can open. Council also asked BC Housing to do a broader round of public consultation.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 3020 Gilmore, participate in BC Housing's upcoming public re-engagement process.

briefs.title: Burnaby plumbing permit fees updated under amended Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed an amendment to its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw that changes the fees charged for plumbing permits. If you pull plumbing permits in Burnaby, expect to pay different amounts going forward.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule before budgeting your next plumbing permit application.

briefs.title: Burnaby updates its rules for how Council meetings are run
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed an amendment to its Procedure Bylaw — the rulebook that governs how Council meetings are conducted. The change received all three required readings, meaning it is now approved.

briefs.title: Rezoning Approved for 8265–8303 10th Avenue — Land Use Rules Now Changed
briefs.summary: Council has given final approval to a zoning bylaw amendment (an official change to the rules about how land can be used) for properties at 8265–8303 10th Avenue. The permitted uses or development potential for these properties has now officially changed.
briefs.action_required: If you own, lease, or plan to develop property on this block of 10th Avenue, review the new zoning rules now.

briefs.title: Burnaby now regulates bear spray sales under its Business Licence Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed Bylaw #14798, adding bear spray sales rules to the Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017. Businesses that sell bear spray in Burnaby must now follow these new licensing requirements.
briefs.action_required: Check if your Burnaby business sells bear spray and confirm you meet the new licence requirements.

briefs.title: Burnaby Bear Spray Bylaw Amendment Passed — New Enforcement Rules Now in Effect
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed an amendment to its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 that adds new rules around bear spray regulation. Businesses that sell or handle bear spray in Burnaby may now be subject to bylaw fines (tickets issued by the city) for non-compliance.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business sells or handles bear spray and review Burnaby's updated bylaw for compliance rules.

briefs.title: Buller Avenue Properties at 7305 & 7317 Move Closer to Rezoning After Council Vote
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first two readings of a bylaw to rezone the properties at 7305 and 7317 Buller Avenue. This moves the rezoning process forward, but final approval has not yet been granted.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 7305–7317 Buller Ave, monitor upcoming council meetings for the public hearing date.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA Bylaw 2026 approved — businesses in the area now affected
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed the Burnaby Business Improvement Area (BIA) Bylaw 2026 for the North Road area. A BIA is a defined business district where local businesses collectively fund area improvements and promotions — and are required to pay a levy (a mandatory fee added to your property tax bill).
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is within the Burnaby North Road BIA boundary and confirm your annual BIA levy amount.

briefs.title: Stoney Creek construction impact measures sent to staff for feasibility review
briefs.summary: City council has referred recommended measures to reduce construction impacts on Stoney Creek to staff for a feasibility review. A report will follow before any action is taken.

briefs.title: Stoney Creek watershed development approvals may be paused pending staff review
briefs.summary: City council has sent a request from the Stoney Creek Environment Committee to staff for a report. The request asks for new watershed protections and a pause on approving new development in the Stoney Creek area.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the staff report outcome if you have active or planned development projects near Stoney Creek.

briefs.title: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw text amendment passes first and second reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council advanced a text amendment (a written change to the rules) to its Zoning Bylaw 1965, completing the first two of three required approval steps. The specific rule changes have not been detailed in the available information.
briefs.action_required: Check the full bylaw text on Burnaby's website to see if your property or business use is affected.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 49-Storey Mixed-Use Tower at Springer Avenue Clears First Council Hurdle
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a rezoning bylaw (a rule change that allows a different type of building on the land) for a 49-storey residential and retail tower at 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue to move to first and second reading. Council also gave early approval to sell City-owned land tied to the project.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near Springer Avenue, monitor future council readings for construction and zoning impacts.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: City approves 2026 active transportation education and promotion programmes
briefs.summary: City council has approved a set of education and promotion initiatives for 2026 aimed at encouraging active transportation (walking, cycling, and other non-motorized travel). No new rules or compliance requirements for businesses were introduced.

briefs.title: City-owned EV charging stations switch to energy-based fees — costs may change for users
briefs.summary: City council approved a new pricing model for public EV charging stations it owns, charging users based on energy consumed (kilowatt-hours used) instead of time parked. Bylaws covering parking meters and fees have been updated to reflect this change.
briefs.action_required: If your business relies on City-owned EV chargers, review the new energy-based rates to update your fleet or employee reimbursement budgets.

briefs.title: 20-Storey Rental Tower at 5058 Imperial Street Moves Forward in Rezoning Process
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved the drafting of a rezoning bylaw (a rule that changes how land can be used) for a 20-storey mixed-use market rental building at 5058 Imperial Street, advancing it to first and second reading.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Village rezoning bylaw advances: taller buildings and new open space rules proposed
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has moved forward a bylaw to update the Burnaby Lake Village Master Plan, changing rules around building height, size, and open space at 6800 Lougheed Highway. The bylaw has passed its first step (first and second reading) and will continue through the approval process.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near 6800 Lougheed Highway, monitor upcoming public hearings to have your say.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Heritage Alteration Permit granted for chimney work at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a Heritage Alteration Permit (official permission to modify a protected historic building) allowing chimney alterations at the W.J. Mathers House 'Altnadene' at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue.

briefs.title: On-Street Public Parking Fees Increased by City Council Decision
briefs.summary: City Council has approved an increase to on-street public pay parking rates. If you or your customers rely on street parking near your business, expect to pay more at the meter.
briefs.action_required: Check the new parking rates and inform customers who regularly use on-street parking near your location.

briefs.title: 84 noise-bylaw-exempt outdoor events approved at Burnaby parks and stadium for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved up to 84 outdoor events at Deer Lake Park, Central Park, and Swangard Stadium that are exempt from the city's noise bylaw (the rule that limits loud sounds in public spaces). These events can run between March 15 and November 1, 2026.
briefs.action_required: If you plan to host or vend at events at these venues in 2026, confirm your event falls within the approved 84-event limit.

key_dates.label: Burnaby noise-bylaw-exempt outdoor event season begins — Deer Lake, Central Park, Swangard
Burnaby noise-bylaw-exempt outdoor event season ends — Deer Lake, Central Park, Swangard

briefs.title: City renews $162,400 portable toilet contract with Jiffy John Rentals
briefs.summary: Council approved a contract renewal with Jiffy John Rentals Ltd. to supply portable toilets at an estimated cost of $162,400 including taxes.

briefs.title: City renews $670,000 security contract with Guardteck for one year
briefs.summary: City council approved a one-year renewal of its security services contract with Guardteck Security Corp, covering officer services at city buildings and special events, valued at approximately $670,000 including taxes.

briefs.title: Burnaby freezes $63,005 FCM membership dues until city gets Big City Mayors' Caucus seat
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has paused its 2026–2027 membership payment of $63,005 to the Federation of Canadian Municipalities (FCM) until the city is given a seat on the Big City Mayors' Caucus, a national group that advocates for large cities.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Option B design for Landmark Burnaby Sign
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Option B design for the Landmark Burnaby Sign and directed staff to move forward with the project.

briefs.title: Antenna variance permit at 9001 University Crescent sent back for further staff review
briefs.summary: A permit application to allow larger antenna equipment on the building at 9001 University Crescent has been paused. City council sent it back to staff for more review before any decision is made.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Technology

briefs.title: Commercial fleet parking and rentals permitted to continue at 7000 Lougheed Highway
briefs.summary: Council renewed the Temporary Use Permit (a time-limited approval allowing a property to be used in a way not normally permitted by zoning rules) for commercial fleet vehicle parking and rentals at 7000 Lougheed Highway.

briefs.title: Lower Westridge construction permits paused pending natural hazard safety assessment
briefs.summary: City council launched a city-wide Natural Hazard Development Permit Area program and approved $120,000 to assess risks in Lower Westridge. New construction permits in Lower Westridge are on hold until the assessment is done.
briefs.action_required: If you plan to build in Lower Westridge, pause permit applications until the hazard assessment is complete.

briefs.title: Utility access right granted over 3860 Sperling Avenue for Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a statutory right-of-way (a legal permission allowing utility companies to access and use part of a property) over a section of 3860 Sperling Avenue to support utility work for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre project.

briefs.title: Antenna variance application at 8888 University Drive sent back for further staff review
briefs.summary: A permit application to allow antenna equipment to exceed normal size and height limits at 8888 University Drive has been sent back to city staff for more review before a decision is made.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA renewed for 2026–2031 with new bylaw coming
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a five-year renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Association (BIA — a group that collects levies from local businesses to fund area improvements) and directed staff to draft a new BIA Bylaw for 2026.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is in the North Road BIA zone — you may owe a new levy starting 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA Bylaw 2026 takes effect — renewal period begins

briefs.title: Burnaby retailers must now follow new rules when selling bear spray
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has changed its business licence rules to regulate how stores can sell bear spray. Retailers in Burnaby must now meet specific requirements under the updated Business Licence Bylaw or face fines.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated Business Licence Bylaw to confirm your bear spray sales practices are compliant.

briefs.title: Plumbing Permit Fees Increased — Check New Costs Before Starting Any Plumbing Work
briefs.summary: City council approved small changes to plumbing permit fees and directed the City Solicitor to write the updated bylaw. If you plan any plumbing work requiring a permit, the fees you pay may be slightly higher than before.
briefs.action_required: Check the updated plumbing permit fee schedule before budgeting your next plumbing project.

briefs.title: City will now remove outdated land restrictions from private properties upon owner request
briefs.summary: The City approved the removal of old restrictive covenants (legal rules tied to a property that limit how it can be used) that it had previously placed on private land. Property owners can now ask the City Solicitor to formally remove these outdated restrictions.
briefs.action_required: If your property has a City-imposed restrictive covenant, contact the City Solicitor's office to request its removal.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail
